comments: Opus 4.7 hit its max_tokens limit of 128K without responding on 31/100 samples. We intend to improve elicitation in the future, and this score should be understood as a lower bound.

All incorrect answers were actually cases of the model failing to submit an answer. For each sample, we average the score of the epochs that did complete for that sample. If none completed, we assign a score of 0. Then we average these scores across all samples. The resulting score is 44/45.
At least one epoch completed for each sample. The scores of each epoch are averaged per sample, and then the scores on each sample are averaged.
1 sample did not complete. The score is reported out of 99.
30 samples did not complete. The score is reported out of 970.
The model had access to a web search tool
10 samples did not complete and were scored as incorrect

In the initial run, 12% of samples failed to complete. Inspect defaults to not scoring these at all, but we override this behavior when >2% of epochs fail. For each sample, we average the score of the epochs that did complete for that sample. If none completed, we assign a score of 0. Then we average these scores across all samples.

In the initial run, 3% of samples failed to complete. Inspect defaults to not scoring these at all, but we override this behavior when >2% of epochs fail. For each sample, we average the score of the epochs that did complete for that sample. If none completed, we assign the random baseline of 25%. Then we average these scores across all samples. In this case, the resulting difference is very small: 93.3% modified vs. 93.9% original.
In the initial run, 1 sample did not complete. This was scored as incorrect.
In the initial run, 8 samples did not complete. These were scored as incorrect.

In the initial run, 3% of samples failed to complete. Inspect defaults to not scoring these at all, but we override this behavior when >2% of epochs fail. For each sample, we average the score of the epochs that did complete for that sample. If none completed, we assign the random baseline of 25%. Then we average these scores across all samples. In this case, the resulting difference is very small: 94.1% modified vs. 94.4% original.

In the initial run, no epochs completed for 2/45 samples. Despite several re-runs of these two samples, no epochs completed. All epochs for the other 43/45 samples were correct. The model's score was edited to be 43/45.
In the initial run, 43 samples did not complete. These were rerun until all but 5 of them completed. The remaining samples are scored as incorrect.
In the initial run, 8 samples did not complete. These were rerun until all but 2 of them completed. The remaining samples are scored as incorrect.
In the initial run, 53 samples did not complete. These were rerun until 51 of them completed. The remaining samples are scored as incorrect.
In the initial run, 9 samples did not complete. These were rerun until 8/9 of them completed. The remaining sample is scored as incorrect.
One sample failed in the initial run. We re-ran that one sample and manually edited the score in this record accordingly.
Two samples did not complete, which is greater than our 2% error tolerance setting. As such, the score has been manually edited to grade them as incorrect.
